title: samolus ebracteatus subsp. cuneatus
intro: Samolus ebracteatus subsp. cuneatus, also known as Chinese marsh clover, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, due to its ability to regulate the body's temperature and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, Samolus ebracteatus subsp. cuneatus is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
